ddls:
CREATE TABLE "test_suites" (
  "test_suite_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test suite */,
  "test_suite_name" TEXT /* Name of the test suite */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test suite */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the test suite belongs to */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the test suite */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the test suite was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the test suite */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the test suite was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_suite_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_suites_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_suites_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_cases" (
  "test_case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case */,
  "test_suite_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test suite the test case belongs to */,
  "test_case_name" TEXT /* Name of the test case */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test case */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the test case belongs to */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the test case (e.g., draft, active, archived) */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority of the test case (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the test case */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the test case was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the test case */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the test case was last modified */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the test case */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_test_suite_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_suite_id") REFERENCES test_suites ("test_suite_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_steps" (
  "test_step_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test step */,
  "test_case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case the test step belongs to */,
  "step_no" INTEGER /* Step number of the test step */,
  "step_description" TEXT /* Description of the test step */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Keyword for the test step (e.g., RLanch, RLogin) */,
  "expected_result" TEXT /* Expected result of the test step */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the test step (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the test step */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the test step was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the test step */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the test step was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_step_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_steps_test_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_case_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("test_case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_steps_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_steps_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_environments" (
  "test_environment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test environment */,
  "test_environment_name" TEXT /* Name of the test environment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test environment */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the test environment */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the test environment was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the test environment */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the test environment was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_environment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_environments_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_environments_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_case_execution" (
  "test_case_execution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case execution */,
  "test_case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case being executed */,
  "test_environment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test environment used for execution */,
  "execution_date" TEXT /* Date when the test case was executed */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the test case execution (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  "executed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who executed the test case */,
  "execution_time" TEXT /* Time when the test case was executed */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or notes about the execution */,
  "automation_tool" TEXT /* Automation tool used for execution (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_case_execution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_case_execution_test_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_case_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("test_case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_case_execution_test_environment_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_environment_id") REFERENCES test_environments ("test_environment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_case_execution_executed_by FOREIGN KEY ("executed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., test engineer, test manager, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date and time when the user joined the system */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_test_cases" (
  "user_test_case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each assignment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user assigned to the test case */,
  "test_case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case assigned to the user */,
  "assigned_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the test case was assigned to the user */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Due date for the test case */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_test_case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_test_cases_test_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_case_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("test_case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_test_cases_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
